patch.value: Nightly windows/Node24: package-manifest SDK-surface exemption uses POSIX endsWith — governance-audit runtime.ts check fails on backslash paths

New nightly red introduced by PR#536 (distinct from pm-b24b init-suite failures on the same leg). tests/unit/packages/package-manifest.spec.ts 'keeps shipped package sources on the public SDK surface' exempts the governance-audit runtime via sourceFile.endsWith("governance-audit/runtime.ts") (spec ~line 862), but sourceFile is built with path.join → on Windows it ends 'governance-audit\runtime.ts', the exemption never matches, and the file falls into the generic public-SDK-import assertion (expects from "@unbrained/pm-cli/sdk", file legitimately imports local ./sdk.ts shim per its resilient-loader design). Spec-side POSIX path assumption class (precedent pm-gf6f); fix = separator-agnostic suffix comparison (compare against path.join(...) or normalize to posix before endsWith). Restoring the windows/Node24 leg to green requires BOTH this and pm-b24b. Consider pinning package-manifest.spec.ts into the windows-regression PR job so spec-side POSIX assumptions are caught pre-merge.
Exemption branch matches on both / and \ separators; nightly windows/Node24 package-manifest test passes; evaluate windows-regression pinning for package-manifest.spec.ts

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Live GitHub evidence 2026-07-14: Nightly Validation run 29310713878 on main dcd58521 includes the expected package-manifest Windows failure alongside pm-b24b's five init/path failures. Other nightly legs passed. Existing canonical item reused; no duplicate and no claim because PR #549 is history/read scoped.

author: codex-root
message: Start wide SDK governance, cross-platform reliability, and release-blocker implementation bundle

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Evidence: the SDK-surface exemption now uses path.join(governance-audit, runtime.ts); package-manifest and workflow tests pass within the 5,721-test exact-coverage gate.

patch.value: Compare the governance runtime suffix with path.join instead of a POSIX literal.
Package-manifest boundary tests recognize governance-audit runtime.ts on Windows.
Native-separator test and required Windows CI workflow contract pass.
Fixed native-separator package-manifest governance detection and added it to required Windows CI.
